name: Mount Agung
eruptionHistory: The 1963 eruption was one of the most devastating of the 20th century, killing over 1,100 people and lowering global temperatures. Renewed activity began in 2017.
description: Mount Agung is the highest point in Bali and a sacred volcano in Balinese Hinduism. The Besakih Temple, Bali's most important Hindu temple, sits on its slopes.

name: Aira Caldera
eruptionHistory: The massive caldera hosts Sakurajima volcano. A catastrophic eruption 22,000 years ago ejected over 150 km³ of material.
description: Aira is a massive caldera in southern Kyushu that formed ~22,000 years ago. Sakurajima volcano sits within the caldera and is one of Japan's most active volcanoes.

name: Alaid
eruptionHistory: The highest volcano in the Kuril Islands. Has erupted multiple times in the 20th century.
description: Alaid is the highest volcano in the Kuril Islands, rising from the sea as a symmetric cone on Atlasov Island.

name: Ambae (Aoba)
eruptionHistory: Major eruptions in 2017-2018 forced complete evacuation of the island's 11,000 residents. Contains an acidic crater lake.
description: Ambae is one of the largest volcanoes in the southwest Pacific. Its 2017-2018 eruptions forced the unprecedented evacuation of the entire island population.

name: Arenal Volcano
eruptionHistory: Erupted nearly continuously from 1968 to 2010. The 1968 eruption destroyed the village of Tabacón and killed 87 people.
description: Arenal was Costa Rica's most active volcano for over four decades. Its near-perfect conical shape and frequent lava flows made it one of the country's top tourist destinations.

name: Mount Asama
eruptionHistory: One of the most active volcanoes on Honshu. The 1783 eruption killed over 1,400 people and produced devastating pyroclastic flows.
description: Mount Asama is one of the most active volcanoes on Japan's main island. Its 1783 Tenmei eruption was one of the most devastating volcanic events in Japanese history.

name: Mount Aso
eruptionHistory: One of the world's most active volcanoes with frequent eruptions. The Naka-dake crater is continuously active. Major caldera-forming eruptions occurred ~90,000 years ago.
description: Mount Aso contains one of the world's largest calderas, measuring 25 km north-south and 18 km east-west. The active Naka-dake crater within the caldera produces frequent phreatic and strombolian eruptions.

name: Aso Caldera
eruptionHistory: Contains one of the world's largest calderas (25x18 km). Multiple large caldera-forming eruptions, the largest ~90,000 years ago.
description: Aso Caldera is one of the largest calderas in the world. The active Naka-dake crater within it is a popular tourist destination despite ongoing volcanic activity.

name: Augustine Volcano
eruptionHistory: Erupts roughly every 10-20 years. Major eruptions in 1883, 1935, 1964, 1976, 1986, and 2006.
description: Augustine is an island stratovolcano in Cook Inlet, Alaska. Its eruptions can generate volcanic tsunamis and disrupt aviation across the North Pacific.

name: Bagana
eruptionHistory: Has been in near-continuous eruption since 1972. One of Melanesia's most active volcanoes.
description: Bagana on Bougainville Island has been in near-continuous effusive eruption since 1972, slowly building one of the youngest volcanic edifices on Earth.

name: Mount Baker
eruptionHistory: Last erupted in 1880. Increased fumarolic activity in 1975 raised concerns but no eruption occurred. Sherman Crater is thermally active.
description: Mount Baker is the most heavily glaciated volcano in the Cascades after Mount Rainier. It produces the most thermal energy of any Cascade volcano.

name: Barren Island
eruptionHistory: The only confirmed active volcano in India. Has been intermittently erupting since 1991.
description: Barren Island is the only confirmed active volcano in the Indian subcontinent. Located in the Andaman Sea, it has been intermittently active since 1991.

name: Bezymianny
eruptionHistory: The 1956 eruption was similar to Mount St. Helens — a massive lateral blast that removed the summit. Has been rebuilding its dome with frequent eruptions since.
description: Bezymianny's catastrophic 1956 eruption provided the template for understanding lateral blast eruptions, later replicated at Mount St. Helens in 1980.

name: Mount Bromo
eruptionHistory: One of Java's most active volcanoes. Located within the Tengger caldera. Regular small eruptions with occasional larger events.
description: Mount Bromo sits within the massive Tengger caldera and is one of Indonesia's most visited volcanoes. The surrounding Sea of Sand creates an otherworldly landscape.

name: Bulusan
eruptionHistory: Frequent phreatic eruptions. Has erupted over 15 times since 1885.
description: Bulusan is one of the most active volcanoes in the Philippines, known for its frequent phreatic (steam-driven) eruptions.

name: Calbuco
eruptionHistory: Erupted dramatically in April 2015 after 43 years of quiet. The explosive eruption produced spectacular lightning-filled ash plumes.
description: Calbuco's stunning 2015 eruption was captured in dramatic photographs showing volcanic lightning. The eruption forced evacuation of thousands in the Los Lagos region.

name: Campi Flegrei
eruptionHistory: A supervolcano caldera. The Campanian Ignimbrite eruption 39,000 years ago was one of the largest in European history. Ongoing bradyseism (ground uplift) since 2005 has raised concern.
description: Campi Flegrei is a supervolcano caldera west of Naples. Ongoing ground uplift and seismic activity have raised alarm about a potential future eruption threatening the Naples metropolitan area.

name: Cerro Azul
eruptionHistory: Has erupted 10 times since 1932. Eruptions are typically effusive, producing lava flows.
description: Cerro Azul is the southwesternmost volcano on Isabela Island in the Galápagos. Its eruptions can threaten unique endemic species.

name: Chaitén
eruptionHistory: Erupted unexpectedly in 2008 after being dormant for over 9,000 years. The eruption forced complete evacuation of the nearby town of Chaitén.
description: Chaitén's 2008 eruption was remarkable for occurring with almost no warning after millennia of dormancy. It produced a massive rhyolite lava dome and destroyed the nearby town.

name: Chikurachki
eruptionHistory: The most active volcano in the northern Kuril Islands with eruptions every few years.
description: Chikurachki is the highest and most active volcano on Paramushir Island in the northern Kuril chain.

name: Mount Cleveland
eruptionHistory: One of the most active volcanoes in the Aleutian arc. Frequent small eruptions with intermittent larger events.
description: Mount Cleveland is an isolated stratovolcano on Chuginadak Island in the central Aleutians. It is one of the most active volcanoes in the Aleutian arc.

name: Volcán de Colima
eruptionHistory: The most active volcano in Mexico. Has erupted more than 40 times since 1576. Major eruptions produce pyroclastic flows and dome collapses.
description: Volcán de Colima, also known as the Volcán de Fuego, is the most historically active volcano in Mexico. It sits within a caldera formed by a massive prehistoric collapse.

name: Cotopaxi
eruptionHistory: Has erupted more than 50 times since 1738. The 1877 eruption produced lahars that traveled over 100 km. Renewed activity began in 2015.
description: Cotopaxi is one of the world's highest active volcanoes. Its glacier-covered summit and massive lahar potential make it one of Ecuador's most dangerous volcanoes.

name: Cumbre Vieja
eruptionHistory: The 2021 eruption on La Palma lasted 85 days and destroyed over 2,900 buildings. Lava flows reached the sea creating new land. Previous eruptions in 1971 and 1949.
description: The 2021 eruption of Cumbre Vieja on La Palma was the most destructive volcanic eruption in the Canary Islands' history, displacing thousands and burying homes, farms, and infrastructure under lava.

name: Deception Island
eruptionHistory: Eruptions in 1967, 1969, and 1970 destroyed scientific stations. The flooded caldera creates a natural harbor.
description: Deception Island is a flooded volcanic caldera that provides a unique natural harbor in Antarctica. Its warm volcanic beaches and abandoned whaling station make it a popular Antarctic tourist stop.

name: Dempo
eruptionHistory: Moderately active with eruptions recorded since 1818.
description: Mount Dempo is one of the highest volcanoes in Sumatra, surrounded by tea plantations in the highlands of South Sumatra.

name: Dieng Volcanic Complex
eruptionHistory: Known for deadly CO2 gas emissions. A 1979 phreatic eruption and gas cloud killed 149 people. Contains numerous craters and geothermal areas.
description: The Dieng Plateau hosts a complex of volcanic craters famous for deadly CO2 gas emissions, colorful volcanic lakes, and ancient Hindu temples.

name: Dukono
eruptionHistory: Has been in near-continuous eruption since 1933. One of Indonesia's most persistently active volcanoes.
description: Dukono has been in almost continuous eruption since 1933, making it one of the most persistently active volcanoes in Indonesia.

name: Ebeko
eruptionHistory: Has been in frequent eruption since 2016. Located near the town of Severo-Kurilsk.
description: Ebeko is the most active volcano on Paramushir Island in the northern Kurils, with frequent explosions that affect the nearby town of Severo-Kurilsk.

name: Mount Erebus
eruptionHistory: Contains the world's southernmost active lava lake. Has been continuously active since at least 1972. Discovered by James Clark Ross in 1841.
description: Mount Erebus is the most active volcano in Antarctica and contains one of the world's few persistent lava lakes. It is the southernmost active volcano on Earth.

name: Erta Ale
eruptionHistory: Contains one of the world's few persistent lava lakes. The lava lake has been active since at least 1967 and possibly much longer.
description: Erta Ale is a continuously active shield volcano in the remote Danakil Depression. It hosts one of the world's few persistent lava lakes, accessible only by multi-day camel trek.

name: Mount Etna
eruptionHistory: One of the world's most active volcanoes with nearly continuous eruption for the past 3,500 years. Major eruptions in 1669, 1928, 1991-93, and frequent summit activity.
description: Mount Etna is Europe's tallest and most active volcano. Its near-constant eruptions have been documented since ancient times, making it one of the best-studied volcanoes in the world.

name: Eyjafjallajökull
eruptionHistory: The 2010 eruption caused unprecedented disruption to European air travel, stranding millions of passengers. Previous eruptions in 1821-1823 and 920 CE.
description: Eyjafjallajökull gained worldwide fame in 2010 when its eruption produced an ash cloud that shut down European airspace for weeks, causing the largest air traffic disruption since World War II.

name: Fagradalsfjall
eruptionHistory: Series of eruptions beginning March 2021 after ~800 years of dormancy on the Reykjanes Peninsula. Multiple eruptions in 2021, 2022, 2023, and 2024.
description: Fagradalsfjall marked the beginning of a new volcanic era on the Reykjanes Peninsula. The accessible eruptions near Reykjavik attracted hundreds of thousands of visitors.

name: La Cumbre (Fernandina)
eruptionHistory: The most active volcano in the Galápagos. Erupts every few years with effusive lava flows. The 1968 eruption caused dramatic caldera collapse.
description: La Cumbre on Fernandina Island is the most volcanically active of the Galápagos volcanoes and sits on one of the most pristine major islands, home to flightless cormorants and marine iguanas.

name: Volcán de Fuego
eruptionHistory: One of Central America's most active volcanoes. The June 2018 eruption produced devastating pyroclastic flows that killed over 190 people in the village of San Miguel Los Lotes.
description: Volcán de Fuego is one of the most active volcanoes in Central America. Its devastating 2018 eruption was one of the deadliest volcanic disasters in Guatemalan history.

name: Mount Fuji
eruptionHistory: Last erupted in 1707 during the Hoei eruption, which deposited ash on Edo (Tokyo). Has erupted at least 16 times since 781 CE.
description: Japan's highest peak and most iconic volcano. Mount Fuji is a near-perfect stratovolcano that has been dormant since 1707. It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a sacred symbol of Japan.

name: Galeras
eruptionHistory: A Decade Volcano. Erupted in 1993 killing 9 people including 6 volcanologists during a field conference. Active since 1580.
description: Galeras is one of the most active volcanoes in Colombia. The 1993 eruption during a scientific conference was a stark reminder of volcanic hazards.

name: Gamalama
eruptionHistory: Has erupted over 70 times since 1538. The city of Ternate is built on its slopes.
description: Gamalama is the volcano that forms the island of Ternate, historically the center of the spice trade. The city of Ternate is built directly on its slopes.

name: Great Sitkin
eruptionHistory: Has been erupting since 2021 with an extensive lava dome/flow in the summit crater.
description: Great Sitkin has been in eruption since 2021, producing one of the most impressive lava flows in recent Aleutian volcanic history.

name: Grímsvötn
eruptionHistory: Iceland's most frequently erupting volcano. Major eruptions in 1996, 1998, 2004, and 2011. The 2011 ash cloud caused flight cancellations.
description: Grímsvötn is Iceland's most active volcano, located beneath the Vatnajökull ice cap. Its eruptions produce glacial floods and ash clouds that can disrupt aviation.

name: Mount Rinjani
eruptionHistory: Contains a stunning crater lake (Segara Anak). The 1257 eruption of nearby Samalas was one of the largest eruptions of the last 10,000 years.
description: Mount Rinjani is Indonesia's second-highest volcano and a popular trekking destination. Its caldera contains the beautiful Segara Anak lake with a small active cone.

name: Big Ben (Heard Island)
eruptionHistory: Has been intermittently active since at least 1910. One of the most remote active volcanoes on Earth.
description: Big Ben on Heard Island is one of the most remote active volcanoes in the world. The glacier-covered stratovolcano is visible mainly via satellite imagery.

name: Hekla
eruptionHistory: Has erupted over 20 times since 874 CE. Medieval Europeans called it the 'Gateway to Hell.' Last erupted in 2000.
description: Hekla was known as the 'Gateway to Hell' in medieval times. It is one of Iceland's most active volcanoes and has erupted frequently throughout the island's settlement history.

name: Mount Hood
eruptionHistory: Last significant eruption around 1781-1782 with a lahar reaching the Columbia River. Minor steam emissions in 1859 and 1865-1866.
description: Mount Hood is Oregon's highest peak and a prominent landmark visible from Portland. While currently dormant, it is considered potentially active with fumaroles near the summit.

name: Cerro Hudson
eruptionHistory: The 1991 eruption was one of the largest in Chile in the 20th century, depositing heavy ashfall across Argentine Patagonia.
description: Cerro Hudson is an ice-filled caldera whose 1991 eruption devastated surrounding areas and deposited heavy ashfall on Argentine Patagonia.

name: Hunga Tonga-Hunga Ha'apai
eruptionHistory: The January 15, 2022 eruption was one of the most powerful volcanic events in recorded history. The blast sent a plume into the mesosphere and generated a global shockwave detectable worldwide.
description: The 2022 Hunga Tonga eruption was the largest atmospheric explosion recorded by modern instruments. It sent water vapor into the stratosphere, generated global tsunamis, and created a sonic boom that circled the Earth multiple times.

name: Ibu
eruptionHistory: Has been erupting frequently since 1998. Produces regular vulcanian explosions and lava dome growth.
description: Mount Ibu has become one of Indonesia's most active volcanoes since its reawakening in 1998, with frequent explosive eruptions and ongoing dome growth.

name: Kanlaon
eruptionHistory: One of the most active volcanoes in the central Philippines. Has erupted over 30 times since 1866.
description: Mount Kanlaon is the most active volcano in the central Philippines and the highest peak in the Visayas. It poses significant hazard to the densely populated province of Negros Occidental.

name: Karangetang
eruptionHistory: One of the most active volcanoes in Indonesia with over 40 eruptions since 1675.
description: Karangetang is one of the most active and dangerous volcanoes in Indonesia, located on the small island of Siau with over 20,000 residents.

name: Karthala
eruptionHistory: One of the most active volcanoes in the Indian Ocean region. Has erupted over 20 times since the 19th century.
description: Karthala dominates the island of Grande Comore and is one of the most active volcanoes in the Indian Ocean.

name: Karymsky
eruptionHistory: Has been in near-continuous vulcanian eruption since 1996. One of Kamchatka's most persistently active volcanoes.
description: Karymsky is the most active volcano of eastern Kamchatka's volcanic arc. It has been in almost continuous eruption since 1996.

name: Katla
eruptionHistory: Has erupted roughly every 40-80 years. The last major eruption was in 1918. It is overdue by historical standards and closely monitored.
description: Katla is one of Iceland's most powerful and feared volcanoes. It lies beneath the Mýrdalsjökull glacier, and eruptions produce massive glacial floods (jökulhlaups) that reshape the landscape.

name: Kelud
eruptionHistory: Known for explosive eruptions. The 2014 eruption destroyed the lava dome that had filled the crater lake. Historical eruptions in 1919 and 1586 were highly destructive.
description: Kelud is one of the most active and dangerous volcanoes in Java. Its crater lake makes eruptions particularly hazardous due to the generation of lahars.

name: Mount Kerinci
eruptionHistory: The highest volcano in Sumatra. Frequent ash emissions and occasional larger eruptions.
description: Mount Kerinci is the highest peak in Sumatra and one of the most active volcanoes in Indonesia. It sits within Kerinci Seblat National Park, home to endangered tigers.

name: Kīlauea
eruptionHistory: One of the most active volcanoes on Earth. Erupted nearly continuously from 1983 to 2018. The 2018 eruption destroyed over 700 homes in Leilani Estates.
description: Kīlauea is one of the world's most active volcanoes and a shield volcano on the southeastern side of the Big Island of Hawaii. Its 2018 eruption was the most destructive in its modern history.

name: Mount Kilimanjaro
eruptionHistory: Last erupted approximately 10,000 years ago. Fumarolic activity in the Kibo summit crater suggests the volcano is not extinct.
description: Africa's highest peak is a dormant stratovolcano with three volcanic cones: Kibo, Mawenzi, and Shira. Its summit glaciers are rapidly shrinking due to climate change.

name: Kirishima
eruptionHistory: The Shinmoe-dake crater erupted dramatically in 2011 and has been intermittently active since. Multiple craters in the complex.
description: Kirishima is a complex of volcanic peaks in southern Kyushu. The Shinmoe-dake crater gained fame when it was featured in the James Bond film 'You Only Live Twice.'

name: Klyuchevskoy
eruptionHistory: The most active volcano in Kamchatka and the highest active volcano in Eurasia. Has erupted over 50 times since 1697.
description: Klyuchevskoy is the highest active volcano in Eurasia and one of the most active in the world. It has been in near-continuous eruption throughout its 6,000-year history.

name: Krakatoa (Anak Krakatau)
eruptionHistory: The 1883 eruption was one of the deadliest in recorded history, killing over 36,000 people and producing a global shockwave. Anak Krakatau emerged in 1927 and collapsed in 2018.
description: Krakatoa is one of the most famous volcanoes in history. The 1883 eruption destroyed most of the island and produced tsunamis up to 30 meters high. The child volcano Anak Krakatau continues to grow and erupt.

name: La Soufrière
location: Caribbean, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines
eruptionHistory: Major eruption in April 2021 forced evacuation of 20,000 people. Previous eruptions in 1979, 1902 (killed 1,680 people), and 1812.
description: La Soufrière's 2021 eruption was its first explosive eruption in over 40 years. The 1902 eruption killed nearly 1,700 people, occurring the same day as the Pelee eruption on Martinique.

name: Langila
eruptionHistory: One of the most frequently active volcanoes in Papua New Guinea with eruptions recorded in most decades.
description: Langila is one of Papua New Guinea's most persistently active volcanoes, located on the northern coast of New Britain.

name: Láscar
eruptionHistory: The most active volcano in the northern Chilean Andes. Major eruption in 1993 produced pyroclastic flows extending 8.5 km from the summit.
description: Láscar is the most active volcano in the northern Chilean Andes, sitting in the extremely arid Atacama Desert at over 5,500 meters elevation.

name: Lewotolo
eruptionHistory: Increased activity since 2020 with frequent ash emissions and occasional larger eruptions.
description: Lewotolo is an active stratovolcano on the island of Lembata in Indonesia's Lesser Sunda Islands. It has become increasingly active in recent years.

name: Llaima
eruptionHistory: One of Chile's most active volcanoes with over 50 historical eruptions. The 2008-2009 eruption produced lava flows and ash columns.
description: Llaima is one of Chile's largest and most active volcanoes. It dominates the landscape of Conguillío National Park with its twin peaks.

name: Lokon-Empung
eruptionHistory: One of the most active volcanoes in North Sulawesi. The Tompaluan crater between the twin peaks is the center of activity.
description: Lokon-Empung is a twin volcano in North Sulawesi. The active crater between the two peaks produces frequent explosive eruptions.

name: Mount Marapi
eruptionHistory: One of the most active volcanoes in Sumatra. An eruption in December 2023 killed 23 hikers near the summit.
description: Mount Marapi is the most active volcano in Sumatra. Not to be confused with Merapi in Java, it has produced numerous eruptions threatening nearby communities.

name: Masaya
eruptionHistory: Contains a persistent lava lake in the Santiago crater. Spanish conquistadors called it 'The Mouth of Hell.' Has been active since 2015.
description: Masaya is one of few volcanoes with a persistent lava lake. Known as 'The Mouth of Hell' since the Spanish colonial era, it is Nicaragua's most accessible volcano.

name: Mauna Loa
eruptionHistory: The world's largest active volcano. Erupted in November 2022 after 38 years of quiet. Has erupted 33 times since 1843.
description: Mauna Loa is the world's largest active volcano by volume and area. When measured from its base on the ocean floor, it rises over 9,000 meters — taller than Mount Everest.

name: Mayon Volcano
eruptionHistory: The most active volcano in the Philippines with over 50 recorded eruptions. The deadliest was in 1814 when pyroclastic flows buried the town of Cagsawa.
description: Mayon is renowned for its near-perfect symmetrical cone shape. It is the most active volcano in the Philippines and one of the most active in the world.

name: Mount Merapi
eruptionHistory: One of the most active and dangerous volcanoes in the world. The 2010 eruption killed 353 people. Has erupted regularly since 1548.
description: Mount Merapi is the most active volcano in Indonesia and one of the most dangerous in the world. It looms over the city of Yogyakarta, threatening millions of people.

name: El Misti
eruptionHistory: Last erupted around 1470 CE. The city of Arequipa (1 million people) sits at its base, making it one of the most dangerous dormant volcanoes.
description: El Misti towers over the city of Arequipa, Peru's second-largest city. Despite dormancy since ~1470, it poses extreme risk due to the large population at its base.

name: Mount Ararat
eruptionHistory: A destructive event in 1840 involved a sector collapse, landslide, and possibly a phreatic eruption that killed thousands. Legendary as Noah's Ark landing site.
description: Mount Ararat is Turkey's highest peak and a symbol of Armenian heritage. Traditionally identified as the landing place of Noah's Ark, it is a dormant volcano with active fumaroles.

name: Mount Cameroon
eruptionHistory: The most active volcano in West Africa. Has erupted 7 times in the last century with lava flows reaching near sea level.
description: Mount Cameroon is the highest peak in West Africa and the most active volcano on the African continent outside the East African Rift.

name: Mount Cleveland
eruptionHistory: One of the most active volcanoes in the Aleutians. Poses significant hazard to aviation.
description: Mount Cleveland on Chuginadak Island is one of the most active and hazardous volcanoes in the Aleutian chain.

name: Mount Damavand
eruptionHistory: Last erupted approximately 7,300 years ago. Active fumaroles near the summit indicate ongoing magmatic activity.
description: Mount Damavand is the highest volcano in Asia and a prominent symbol in Persian mythology. Active fumaroles suggest it is dormant rather than extinct.

name: Manam
eruptionHistory: One of Papua New Guinea's most active volcanoes. Major eruption in 2004 forced evacuation of the entire island population.
description: Manam is one of the most active volcanoes in Papua New Guinea. The 2004 eruption displaced the entire island population of about 10,000 people.

name: Mount Michael
eruptionHistory: Contains one of the world's rarest persistent lava lakes, confirmed by satellite in 2019.
description: Mount Michael on Saunders Island hosts one of the world's few persistent lava lakes, discovered by satellite in one of Earth's most remote locations.

name: Mount Pagan
eruptionHistory: The island was evacuated in 1981 due to a major eruption and has remained uninhabited since.
description: Pagan Island in the Northern Mariana Islands was evacuated in 1981 after a major eruption. The island remains uninhabited due to ongoing volcanic activity.

name: Mount Spurr
eruptionHistory: Last erupted in 1992 from the Crater Peak vent, sending ash over Anchorage. Previous eruption in 1953.
description: Mount Spurr is the highest volcano in the Aleutian Range. Its 1992 eruption deposited ash on Anchorage and disrupted aviation.

name: Nevado del Huila
eruptionHistory: Reawakened in 2007 after centuries of dormancy. Multiple eruptions and lahars through 2012.
description: Nevado del Huila is the highest active volcano in Colombia. Its reactivation in 2007 after centuries of quiet produced multiple lahars threatening downstream communities.

name: Nevado del Ruiz
eruptionHistory: The 1985 eruption triggered a lahar that destroyed the town of Armero, killing approximately 23,000 people — one of the deadliest volcanic disasters in history.
description: Nevado del Ruiz is infamous for the 1985 Armero tragedy, when lahars from a small eruption killed over 23,000 people. It remains one of the most dangerous volcanoes in Colombia.

name: Ojos del Salado
eruptionHistory: The world's highest active volcano. Evidence of eruption about 1,000-1,500 years ago. Active fumaroles near the summit.
description: Ojos del Salado is the highest active volcano in the world at 6,893 meters. Its summit hosts the world's highest lake and active sulfur fumaroles.

name: Nevados de Chillán
eruptionHistory: Has been in eruption since 2016 with dome growth, explosions, and occasional pyroclastic flows.
description: Nevados de Chillán has been in a prolonged eruption since 2016, building a large lava dome and producing frequent explosions. It is a popular ski destination despite the volcanic activity.

name: Nishinoshima
eruptionHistory: Emerged as a new volcanic island in 2013 and has been growing continuously through repeated eruptions.
description: Nishinoshima is a volcanic island that dramatically expanded beginning in 2013, providing a real-time example of island building through volcanic eruption.

name: Nyamuragira
location: East Africa, Democratic Republic of Congo
eruptionHistory: Africa's most active volcano. Has erupted more than 30 times since 1882. Produced a lava lake in its summit crater in 2012.
description: Nyamuragira is the most active volcano in Africa, erupting roughly every 2-4 years. Its lava flows have repeatedly threatened Virunga National Park and mountain gorilla habitat.

name: Mount Nyiragongo
location: East Africa, Democratic Republic of Congo
eruptionHistory: Contains one of the world's rare persistent lava lakes. The 2002 eruption destroyed much of the city of Goma. Erupted again in 2021 killing 32 people.
description: Nyiragongo contains the world's largest lava lake in its summit crater. Its extremely fluid lava can flow at speeds up to 100 km/h, making it one of the most dangerous volcanoes in Africa.

name: Ol Doinyo Lengai
eruptionHistory: The world's only active volcano that erupts natrocarbonatite lava. Major explosive eruption in 2007-2008 changed the summit profile.
description: Ol Doinyo Lengai is unique as the only active volcano erupting natrocarbonatite lava — carbonatite lava that is much cooler (500°C) and more fluid than silicate lavas.

name: Mount Ontake
eruptionHistory: A sudden phreatic eruption on September 27, 2014 killed 63 hikers — Japan's worst volcanic disaster since 1926.
description: Mount Ontake's tragic 2014 eruption occurred without warning while hundreds of hikers were on the summit. It highlighted the unpredictable danger of phreatic eruptions.

name: Osorno
eruptionHistory: Last erupted in 1869. Has produced at least 11 historical eruptions since 1575.
description: Osorno is one of the most visually striking volcanoes in Chile, often compared to Mount Fuji for its symmetrical cone shape. It is a major ski resort and tourist attraction.

name: Pacaya
eruptionHistory: Has been in near-continuous eruption since 1961. One of Guatemala's most active volcanoes.
description: Pacaya is one of Guatemala's most active and accessible volcanoes. Its frequent eruptions and proximity to Guatemala City make it a popular tourist destination.

name: Pavlof Volcano
eruptionHistory: One of the most active volcanoes in the Aleutian arc. Has erupted over 40 times since 1790.
description: Pavlof is one of the most consistently active volcanoes in the Aleutian arc. It is a symmetrical stratovolcano visible from the community of Cold Bay.

name: Mount Pelée
eruptionHistory: The 1902 eruption destroyed the city of Saint-Pierre, killing approximately 29,000 people — the deadliest volcanic eruption of the 20th century. Small eruption in 1929-1932.
description: Mount Pelée's 1902 eruption was the deadliest of the 20th century. The pyroclastic flow that destroyed Saint-Pierre in minutes left only 2-3 survivors and introduced the term 'nuée ardente.'

name: Mount Pinatubo
eruptionHistory: The June 1991 eruption was the second largest of the 20th century (VEI 6). It ejected ~10 km³ of material and lowered global temperatures by 0.5°C for a year.
description: Mount Pinatubo's cataclysmic 1991 eruption was one of the largest and most well-studied eruptions of the 20th century. The eruption produced massive pyroclastic flows and a global climate impact.

name: Piton de la Fournaise
eruptionHistory: One of the world's most active volcanoes. Erupts on average every 8 months. Eruptions are typically effusive and rarely dangerous.
description: Piton de la Fournaise is one of the world's most active and accessible volcanoes. Its frequent, non-explosive eruptions make it a popular tourist attraction on Réunion Island.

name: Piton des Neiges
eruptionHistory: Last erupted approximately 12,000 years ago. Now deeply eroded with three spectacular amphitheater-like cirques.
description: Piton des Neiges is the highest point on Réunion Island and the highest peak in the Indian Ocean. The extinct volcano's three dramatic cirques are a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Popocatépetl
eruptionHistory: Has been in near-continuous activity since 1994 after 70 years of dormancy. Frequent vulcanian explosions and dome growth/collapse cycles.
description: Popocatépetl is one of Mexico's most active volcanoes, towering over the Mexico City metropolitan area. Over 25 million people live within potential hazard zones.

name: Puyehue-Cordón Caulle
eruptionHistory: The 2011-2012 eruption produced massive ash clouds that disrupted aviation across the southern hemisphere and deposited heavy ashfall on Argentine Patagonia.
description: The 2011 eruption of Puyehue-Cordón Caulle was one of the most significant Chilean eruptions in decades, producing rhyolite lava flows and ash that affected Argentina and beyond.

name: Rabaul (Tavurvur)
eruptionHistory: Twin eruptions of Tavurvur and Vulcan in 1994 destroyed much of Rabaul town. The caldera has hosted several major eruptions including a devastating 1937 event.
description: Rabaul caldera contains some of the most active volcanoes in Papua New Guinea. The simultaneous 1994 eruptions of Tavurvur and Vulcan destroyed the town of Rabaul.

name: Mount Rainier
eruptionHistory: Last confirmed eruption in the 1890s. Considered one of the most dangerous volcanoes in the US due to massive glacier-fed lahar potential threatening Tacoma and Seattle suburbs.
description: Mount Rainier is an active stratovolcano and the most glaciated peak in the contiguous US. It is classified as a Decade Volcano due to the extreme risk lahars pose to nearby populated areas.

name: Mount Redoubt
eruptionHistory: Major eruptions in 1902, 1966, 1989-90, and 2009. The 1989 eruption caused a KLM 747 to lose all four engines.
description: Mount Redoubt is a prominent stratovolcano on the western side of Cook Inlet. Its eruptions pose significant hazards to aviation and the Kenai Peninsula.

name: Reventador
eruptionHistory: Major eruption in 2002 sent ash to Quito. Has been in near-continuous eruption since 2002.
description: El Reventador is Ecuador's most active volcano, located in the Amazon foothills east of the Andes. Its 2002 eruption deposited ash on the capital city of Quito.

name: Rincón de la Vieja
eruptionHistory: Frequent phreatic eruptions from the active crater lake. Has erupted over 15 times since 1851.
description: Rincón de la Vieja is one of Costa Rica's most active volcanoes, featuring hot springs, mud pots, and an active crater lake.

name: Mount Ruapehu
eruptionHistory: Most active volcano in New Zealand. The 1953 Tangiwai disaster killed 151 when a lahar from the crater lake destroyed a railway bridge.
description: Mount Ruapehu is New Zealand's largest active volcano and a popular ski resort. Its summit crater lake periodically produces lahars during eruptions.

name: Sabancaya
eruptionHistory: Has been in continuous eruption since 2016 with daily ash emissions.
description: Sabancaya has been erupting since 2016, producing daily ash plumes that affect communities in the Colca Valley region.

name: Minamidake (Sakurajima)
eruptionHistory: The Minamidake summit crater is the most active vent of Sakurajima, producing hundreds to thousands of explosive events per year.
description: Minamidake is the most active summit crater of Sakurajima volcano, producing near-daily explosions that shower ash on Kagoshima city.

name: Sakurajima
eruptionHistory: One of the most active volcanoes in the world. Has been in near-continuous eruption since 1955. The 1914 eruption was the most powerful in 20th century Japan.
description: Sakurajima is one of Japan's most active volcanoes, located in Kagoshima Bay. It was formerly an island but became connected to the Osumi Peninsula by lava flows from the 1914 eruption.

name: Sangay
eruptionHistory: One of the most active volcanoes in Ecuador, erupting almost continuously since 1934.
description: Sangay has been in near-continuous eruption since 1934, making it one of the most persistently active volcanoes in the Andes.

name: Sangeang Api
eruptionHistory: The 2014 eruption produced spectacular ash plumes photographed from aircraft. Has erupted numerous times since 1512.
description: Sangeang Api is a small volcanic island north of Sumbawa. Its 2014 eruption produced dramatic ash columns captured in widely shared photographs.

name: Santa María
eruptionHistory: The 1902 eruption was one of the largest of the 20th century (VEI 6). The Santiaguito dome complex has been actively erupting in its crater since 1922.
description: Santa María's devastating 1902 eruption was one of the four largest volcanic eruptions of the 20th century. Its crater now hosts the actively growing Santiaguito dome complex.

name: Santiaguito
eruptionHistory: Has been continuously erupting since 1922, growing a complex of lava domes in the crater of Santa María volcano.
description: Santiaguito is a volcanic dome complex that has been growing continuously since 1922 in the crater formed by Santa María's catastrophic 1902 eruption.

name: Santorini (Thera)
eruptionHistory: The Minoan eruption ~1600 BCE was one of the largest in the last 10,000 years, possibly destroying the Minoan civilization. Nea Kameni has erupted several times in the last 2,000 years.
description: Santorini's caldera was formed by the massive Minoan eruption around 1600 BCE. The eruption may have inspired the legend of Atlantis and contributed to the decline of the Minoan civilization.

name: Mount Semeru
eruptionHistory: The highest volcano in Java. Has been in near-continuous eruption since 1967. A major eruption in December 2021 killed over 50 people.
description: Mount Semeru is Java's highest peak and one of the most active volcanoes in Indonesia. It is named after Meru, the central mountain of the universe in Hindu-Buddhist cosmology.

name: Semisopochnoi
eruptionHistory: Has been in intermittent eruption since 2018 from the North Cerberus crater.
description: Semisopochnoi is the largest volcanic island in the western Aleutians. It contains a caldera with several active volcanic cones.

name: Mount Shasta
eruptionHistory: Last erupted around 1250 CE. Has averaged one eruption every 600 years over the last 10,000 years. Significant lahar and pyroclastic flow hazard.
description: Mount Shasta is one of the largest stratovolcanoes in the Cascades. Its glaciers and massive bulk make it a prominent feature of the northern California landscape.

name: Shishaldin Volcano
eruptionHistory: One of the most active Aleutian volcanoes. The 2023 eruption produced lava flows and ash plumes over 40,000 feet.
description: Shishaldin is the highest peak in the Aleutian Islands and has the most symmetrical cone of any Aleutian volcano. Its near-constant steam plume is a prominent landmark.

name: Shiveluch
eruptionHistory: One of the most active volcanoes in Kamchatka. The April 2023 eruption produced the highest ash plume in decades, reaching 20 km altitude.
description: Shiveluch is one of Kamchatka's largest and most active volcanoes. Its massive 2023 eruption sent ash across the Pacific and caused widespread disruption.

name: Sierra Negra
eruptionHistory: Has one of the largest calderas in the Galápagos. Erupted in 2005 and 2018 with extensive lava flows.
description: Sierra Negra on Isabela Island has one of the largest basaltic calderas in the world. Its eruptions produce impressive lava flows on the Galápagos landscape.

name: Mount Sinabung
eruptionHistory: Dormant for over 400 years before reawakening in 2010. Has been in near-continuous eruption since 2013. Produced large pyroclastic flows in 2014 and 2016.
description: Mount Sinabung was dormant for over four centuries before its dramatic reawakening in 2010. It has since become one of Indonesia's most persistently active volcanoes.

name: Mount Slamet
eruptionHistory: The second-highest volcano in Java. Has erupted over 20 times since 1772.
description: Mount Slamet is the second-highest peak in Java and one of its most active volcanoes, with a history of frequent eruptions.

name: Soputan
eruptionHistory: One of the most active volcanoes in Sulawesi. Has erupted frequently since 1785.
description: Mount Soputan is one of the most active volcanoes in North Sulawesi, with frequent explosive eruptions.

name: Soufrière Hills
eruptionHistory: Began erupting in 1995 after centuries of dormancy. Pyroclastic flows destroyed the capital Plymouth in 1997, rendering the southern half of the island uninhabitable.
description: The eruption of Soufrière Hills volcano has transformed Montserrat. The destruction of Plymouth made it the only modern capital city to be abandoned due to volcanic activity.

name: Mount St. Helens
eruptionHistory: The catastrophic May 18, 1980 eruption killed 57 people and devastated 600 km² of forest. A lateral blast traveling 1,080 km/h removed the mountain's north face. Dome-building continued until 2008.
description: Mount St. Helens is the most active volcano in the Cascades. Its devastating 1980 eruption remains the most significant volcanic event in US history, providing invaluable data on lateral blast dynamics.

name: Stromboli
eruptionHistory: Has been in near-continuous eruption for at least 2,000 years. Regular strombolian explosions every 10-20 minutes give the volcano its iconic status.
description: Stromboli has been erupting almost continuously for over 2,000 years, earning it the name 'Lighthouse of the Mediterranean.' Its regular explosive bursts define the 'strombolian' eruption type.

name: Ioto (Iwo Jima)
eruptionHistory: The island is rising at one of the fastest rates on Earth — over 1 meter per decade. Submarine eruptions occur periodically.
description: Ioto (Iwo Jima), site of the famous WWII battle, sits atop an actively rising volcanic caldera. The island is uplifting so rapidly that WWII-era beaches are now high above sea level.

name: Surtsey
eruptionHistory: Born from a submarine eruption in 1963-1967, creating a new island. A UNESCO World Heritage Site studied as a pristine example of ecological colonization.
description: Surtsey is a volcanic island that emerged from the Atlantic Ocean in 1963. It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the most important natural laboratories for studying island biogeography.

name: Suwanosejima
eruptionHistory: One of Japan's most active volcanoes. Has been in frequent eruption since 1949.
description: Suwanosejima is one of the most active volcanoes in Japan's Ryukyu Islands. Its Otake crater produces frequent strombolian and vulcanian eruptions.

name: Taal Volcano
eruptionHistory: Has erupted at least 34 times since 1572. The 1911 eruption killed 1,335 people. January 2020 eruption forced evacuation of hundreds of thousands.
description: Taal is one of the smallest and most active volcanoes in the Philippines. It sits within a lake inside a larger caldera, creating a dramatic 'volcano within a lake within a volcano' setting.

name: Mount Tambora
eruptionHistory: The 1815 eruption was the largest in recorded history (VEI 7). It killed over 71,000 people and caused the 'Year Without a Summer' in 1816 with global crop failures.
description: Mount Tambora's 1815 eruption was the most powerful volcanic eruption in recorded human history. It reduced the mountain's height from ~4,300m to 2,850m and created a 6km-wide caldera.

name: Taupō
eruptionHistory: The 232 CE Hatepe eruption was one of the most violent eruptions in the last 5,000 years. A supereruption ~26,500 years ago (Oruanui) ejected 1,170 km³ of material.
description: Lake Taupō fills a massive volcanic caldera. The Oruanui supereruption ~26,500 years ago was one of the largest on Earth in the last 70,000 years.

name: Mount Teide
eruptionHistory: Last eruption in 1909 was from a vent on the northwest flank. Historical eruptions in 1798 and 1706. A Decade Volcano due to proximity to populated areas.
description: Mount Teide is the highest peak in Spain and the third-tallest volcanic structure in the world from its base. It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and Spain's most visited natural attraction.

name: Tolbachik
eruptionHistory: The 2012-2013 flank eruption produced extensive lava flows. The 1975-1976 'Great Tolbachik Fissure Eruption' was one of the largest basaltic eruptions of the 20th century.
description: Tolbachik is a large volcanic massif consisting of two overlapping stratovolcanoes. Its effusive eruptions produce spectacular lava flows visible from space.

name: Tongariro
eruptionHistory: The 2012 eruption from Te Maari crater was the first in over a century. Historical eruptions include 1896 and earlier.
description: Tongariro is a complex volcanic massif with multiple craters. It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and home to the famous Tongariro Alpine Crossing track.

name: Tungurahua
eruptionHistory: Reactivated in 1999 after 80 years of dormancy. Was in intermittent eruption from 1999 to 2016 with periodic explosions and pyroclastic flows.
description: Tungurahua, meaning 'Throat of Fire' in Quechua, rises dramatically above the city of Baños. Its long eruption cycle from 1999 to 2016 required periodic evacuations.

name: Irazú
eruptionHistory: The 1963-1965 eruption coincided with President Kennedy's visit to Costa Rica. Ashfall reached San José and destroyed crops.
description: Irazú is the highest active volcano in Costa Rica. Its summit crater lake and lunar-like landscape are popular tourist attractions.

name: Turrialba
eruptionHistory: Reactivated in 2010 after 150 years of quiet. Frequent ash emissions through 2020.
description: Turrialba reawakened in 2010 and has been producing frequent ash emissions that occasionally reach the capital San José.

name: Ubinas
eruptionHistory: Peru's most active volcano. Has erupted over 25 times since 1550.
description: Ubinas is the most active volcano in Peru, with a long history of eruptions that periodically displace communities in the surrounding valleys.

name: Ulawun
eruptionHistory: One of the most active and dangerous volcanoes in Papua New Guinea. A Decade Volcano due to its explosive potential and proximity to populated areas.
description: Ulawun is the highest volcano in the Bismarck Archipelago and one of the most dangerous in Papua New Guinea. It is designated a Decade Volcano due to its history of large eruptions.

name: Mount Unzen
eruptionHistory: The 1991-1995 eruption produced a lava dome and devastating pyroclastic flows that killed 43 people including volcanologists. The 1792 eruption triggered a tsunami killing ~15,000.
description: Mount Unzen is a group of stratovolcanoes on the Shimabara Peninsula. The 1792 sector collapse and resulting tsunami was the worst volcanic disaster in Japan's history.

name: Mount Veniaminof
eruptionHistory: One of the largest and most active volcanoes in the Aleutian arc. Contains an 8 km caldera with an ice-filled cone.
description: Mount Veniaminof is one of the largest volcanoes on the Alaska Peninsula, with a massive ice-filled caldera. Eruptions melt through the glacier producing steam and ash.

name: Mount Vesuvius
eruptionHistory: Destroyed Pompeii and Herculaneum in 79 CE (VEI 5). Last erupted in 1944. Over 3 million people live in the red zone surrounding the volcano.
description: Vesuvius is one of the most famous and dangerous volcanoes in the world. Its 79 CE eruption buried the Roman cities of Pompeii and Herculaneum and is one of the most documented volcanic disasters in history.

name: Villarrica
eruptionHistory: One of Chile's most active volcanoes with over 60 historical eruptions. Contains a rare persistent lava lake in its summit crater.
description: Villarrica is one of only a few volcanoes worldwide with a persistent lava lake. It is a major ski resort and tourist destination despite its frequent activity.

name: Vulcano
eruptionHistory: Last erupted 1888-1890. The word 'volcano' itself derives from this island's Latin name Vulcanus. Active fumaroles and hydrothermal vents.
description: Vulcano gave its name to all volcanoes. The island has been a natural laboratory for studying volcanic phenomena since Roman times.

name: Whakaari
eruptionHistory: Frequent phreatic eruptions. The 2019 eruption during tourist visit killed 22 people.
description: New Zealand's most active cone volcano, known for deadly 2019 tourist eruption.

name: Whakaari
eruptionHistory: Frequent phreatic eruptions. December 2019 eruption killed 22 tourists on the island.
description: New Zealand's most active cone volcano. The 2019 disaster led to changes in volcanic tourism safety regulations worldwide.

name: Whakaari / White Island
eruptionHistory: Erupted on December 9, 2019 while 47 tourists were on the island, killing 22 people. Has been intermittently active throughout historical record.
description: Whakaari/White Island is New Zealand's most active cone volcano. The tragic 2019 eruption during a tourist visit highlighted the dangers of visiting active volcanoes.

name: Wolf Volcano
eruptionHistory: The highest volcano in the Galápagos. Erupted in 2015 and 2022. The 2015 eruption threatened the habitat of the only population of pink iguanas.
description: Wolf Volcano is the highest point in the Galápagos Islands. It is home to the only population of Galápagos pink land iguanas, threatened by volcanic eruptions.

name: Yasur
eruptionHistory: Has been in near-continuous eruption for at least 800 years. One of the most accessible active volcanoes in the world.
description: Yasur is one of the world's most accessible active volcanoes, allowing visitors to stand at the crater rim and watch strombolian explosions. It has been erupting for at least 800 years.
